summary: Missing cranelift core codegen spec (the most relevant spec for the recursion bug). Inaccurate key requirement mappings for builtins (R4/R5 do not cover print) and string-ops (R1 does not cover mb_str_concat). builtins missing from Spec Plan.

findings.detail: Missing cranelift core spec (codegen/cranelift.md). R2 (MIR Instruction Translation — function calls, NaN-boxing) and R4 (Value Marshaling — return value conversion) are directly relevant to the recursion bug (Q2) and binary op dispatch (Q3). cranelift-jit only covers JIT module lifecycle, not code generation.

builtins key requirements listed as R4 (Conversion Builtins) and R5 (Enhanced P1 Builtins), but neither covers print(). mb_print exists in builtins.rs but has no corresponding requirement in the spec. The R4/R5 mapping is factually incorrect.
string-ops key requirement listed as R1 (Core String Methods), but R1 covers methods like split/join/strip — not the + operator concatenation. mb_str_concat is in string_ops.rs but is not part of R1.
builtins appears in the reference table (medium relevance) but is missing from the Spec Plan section. If mb_print needs fixing to return TAG_NONE, builtins should be in the plan.

required_changes: Add cranelift (codegen/cranelift.md) as high relevance with key requirements R2, R4
Fix builtins key requirements — R4/R5 do not cover print(); note that print() has no existing requirement (spec gap)
Fix string-ops key requirement — R1 does not cover mb_str_concat (spec gap for concatenation operator)
Add builtins to Spec Plan if mb_print return value needs modification
